<!-- Thanks for contributing! Please fill this out before submitting. -->

## Summary

<!-- 1-3 sentence summary of the change -->

## Type of change

- [ ] New promise (content)
- [ ] New incident (content)
- [ ] Update / correction to existing entry
- [ ] Feature (code)
- [ ] Bug fix (code)
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Refactor / tooling

## Content checklist (skip if pure code PR)

- [ ] Every claim in `summary` is backed by at least one source
- [ ] `evidence_grade` is honestly chosen (no inflating "Allegation" to "Multiple Sources")
- [ ] `current_status` reflects state at time of PR, not aspirational
- [ ] Sources include date + outlet + URL (live, not paywalled-only)
- [ ] No personally-identifying info about private citizens
- [ ] Politicians named only with source-documented basis (allegations marked as such)

## Code checklist (skip if pure data PR)

- [ ] `npm run validate` passes
- [ ] `npm run test:unit` passes
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] No new dependencies without justification
- [ ] No `console.log` left in production code
- [ ] No hardcoded credentials, API keys, or DB URLs

## Related issues

<!-- "Closes #123" or "Refs #456" -->

## Screenshots (if UI change)

<!-- Drag images here -->

## Editorial conflicts of interest (optional)

<!-- Disclose if you have any direct stake in the content — e.g. you're a candidate's staff,
     a journalist who covered this story, a researcher with funding from a related entity.
     Disclosure is not disqualifying — it helps reviewers weigh perspective. -->
